meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no SaaS knowledge and professional freelance success. A step-by-step guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of experience into a competitive advantage by building 'proof-of-concept' prototypes. Use Antigravity to scaffold production-ready backends and Claude Code to iterate on client requirements instantly. By pitching small, high-impact features rather than full platforms, you build a portfolio while earning your first freelance revenue.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, paralyzed by the 'SaaS void'—that gap between knowing how to code and knowing how to build a product that people actually pay for. You have the ambition, but the roadmap seems buried in layers of complex architecture and overwhelming documentation. It feels like you're trying to build a skyscraper without a blueprint, and the impostor syndrome is hitting hard.

But here is the secret: you don't need a decade of experience to land your first client. You need a fast, iterative workflow that turns abstract ideas into tangible demos. By leveraging modern AI-assisted development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can compress months of learning into a single weekend of execution. Let’s turn that 'no knowledge' label into your first paid project.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Problem
steps.description: Don't aim for a SaaS platform; aim for a single, high-value feature. Identify a repetitive task for a business owner.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a product strategist. List 5 high-value micro-SaaS features that automate manual data entry for local businesses. Focus on simplicity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project spec for a simple automated email parser for small business owners'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. Focus on a clean, scalable structure that allows for rapid feature expansion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust FastAPI backend structure with SQLite for local storage and Pydantic models for data validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a FastAPI project structure with requirements.txt and basic CRUD endpoints'

steps.title: Build the Core Logic
steps.description: Implement the core value proposition using Claude Code. Focus on writing testable, modular code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean service layer that processes incoming JSON webhooks and saves them to a database. Include error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the webhook service logic to parse incoming JSON payloads into the database'

steps.title: Craft the Client Pitch
steps.description: Translate your technical work into business value. Your pitch should focus on ROI, not the stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a persuasive 150-word email pitch for a local business owner explaining how this automated tool saves them 5 hours a week.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Draft a cold email sequence for a freelance developer pitching an automation tool to a local business'

steps.title: Deployment and Demonstration
steps.description: Deploy your prototype to a free-tier hosting provider so the client can interact with it immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Dockerfile and a deployment script for Railway or Render to get this MVP live in minutes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a Dockerfile and deployment configuration for a Python FastAPI application'

pro_tips: Always pitch the time saved, not the tech stack used.
Keep your MVP under 200 lines of code; complexity is the enemy of the first sale.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; it makes future maintenance for the client effortless.
Focus on 'Read-Only' demos first—let them see the data before they pay for the write-access.
Record a 60-second Loom video of your working prototype; it is more effective than a 5-page proposal.
content.next_steps: Once you have your first prototype live, reach out to three local business owners for feedback. Do not ask them to buy; ask them if the tool solves their specific workflow issue.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the strategy to stop being a student and start being a builder. Stop waiting for permission to enter the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
